body:
tungsten-forge PASS 2 -- SLS(30) / 9.44

Trigger: Manuel's live USDJPY M5 calibration of SLS(29) completed for real -- CALIBRATION RESULT: STAND DOWN, cause OVERFIT. Full 93.5min Journal analysed.

Diagnosis: the STAND DOWN is correct behavior (Stage 10: a ghost refusal is the architecture working), not a bug. All 9 regimes independently showed the same shape -- good train WR, holdout collapse of -19pp to -44pp, enh-check correctly flagged overfit, correctly fell back to equal weights. Root lead: Phase 3 logged 'Total combos: 562500' searched against one train/holdout split with no correction sized to that count and no multi-window walk-forward (SS-III.6) -- a likely shared cause behind all 9 regimes showing the identical failure shape, not 9 independent non-findings.

Did NOT force a different outcome out of this data -- would violate Standing Prohibition #2. Fixed for real instead:
- WARRANT#9: the n<500 Power Doctrine floor (open since pass 1) -- live evidence directly justified it: regimes r3/r4/r8 (n=387/140/150) were computing/shrinking ICs the MDE table says can't resolve anything. Gate raised n<30 -> n<500.
- Lesson#4 fixed: SLS(24) hardcoded version-string bug -- new EA_BUILD_ID constant, all 8 Print sites now derive from one place instead of 8 stale literals.
- Structural parity (H6) verified unchanged.

Deferred, not fixed: redesigning Phase 3B's search methodology (proper correction/walk-forward) -- bigger undertaking, needs its own warrant, logged as hypothesis #3 for pass 3.

SLS(30).mq4 attached, not yet compiled or backtested.
